Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records
Off Calendar Oct-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Juan Flores, Esq. 415-495-7000 Arns Davis Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Philip Downs, Esq. 650-871-9544 Cholakian & Associates for Defendant Mission Constructors.
David Hatch, Esq. 866-286-5002 Plain Legal for Defendant/moving party Isabelle Concio.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Argued. Parties discuss subpoeanas that are withdrawn. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ISABELLE CONCIO's Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendant Isabelle Aquino Concio's Notice of Motion and Motion to Quash Plaintiffs' Deposition Subpoena for Production of Business Records (to Bank of America and Citizens Business Bank); Protective Order; and Compensatory Sanctions against Plaintiffs in the Amount of $6,750 is GRANTED and DENIED as follows. The request to quash the subpoena is denied as the subpoena was never served on third party subpoena recipients and was withdrawn by Plaintiffs' counsel. See Decl., of Flores, paragraph 4. The request for protective order is denied as there are no subpoenas pending that are the subject of this motion. The request for sanctions for abuse of the discovery process is granted. On Wednesday, August 31, 2023, Plaintiffs prepared and served by mail the third party subpoenas (apparently through Republic Document Management). Decl., of Flores, paragraph 3; Decl., of Whitemyer, Exs. 1-2. On Wednesday, September 6, 2023, counsel for Defendant Aquino Concio met and conferred with Plaintiffs' counsel on the subpoenas, stating, in relevant part, "If you do not advise me and all recipients of your subpoenas for our client's records by 10am tomorrow of your immediate withdrawal of your subpoenas for our clients' records, we will swiftly move the court for an Order?" It does not appear that Plaintiffs' counsel responded with any agreement to withdraw the subpoenas or even informed Defendant's counsel that the subpoenas had not been served on the third parties. The Court notes that no notices to consumer was served with the subpoenas. Sanctions in the amount of $1,000 against Plaintiffs' counsel and in favor of Defendant Isabelle Aquino Concio are granted for abuse of discovery. Sanctions are due and payable within one week of notice of this order.

Parties to meet and confer on proposed order. Parties to submit a Proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in word format before noon today to the Judge Pro Tem at Peter.Catalanotti@wilsonelser.com. In addition, the agreement of parties to withdraw subpoenas shall also be included.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
